input.initialPrompt: Analyze this project for incremental build issues. Identify any custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to analyze the project for incremental build issues. Let me look at the project files to understand what's there.

1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

     </PropertyGroup>

       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
     </Exec>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                      Overwrite="true" />

     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)

**Problem:** Writes the current time (`DateTime::Now`) to a file on every build. Since the timestamp always changes, it produces a new file each run, which invalidates downstream targets (like `CoreCompile`) even when source code hasn't changed.

**Also missing:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes on the target, so MSBuild can never skip it.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)

**Problem:** Runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes the result to `git-hash.g.cs` **unconditionally** every build. Even if the git hash hasn't changed, it overwrites the file (new timestamp on disk), triggering recompilation.

**Also missing:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild always re-executes this target.

---

### Recommended Fixes

| Target | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Remove it, or add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and use a deterministic value. A volatile value like `DateTime.Now` fundamentally cannot be incremental. |
| `EmbedGitHash` | Add `Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFile)"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"`. Additionally, compare the new hash with the existing file and only overwrite when it changes (e.g., read existing, compare, then write). |

Both targets lack the `Inputs`/`Outputs` contract that MSBuild needs to determine "nothing changed — skip this target," so they unconditionally re-execute and force a full recompilation every time.
